steps.title: Set up the scale ratio
steps.content: The scale gives $1$ inch (model) $= \frac{19}{4}$ feet (actual). Equivalently, $\frac{19}{4}$ feet of actual length corresponds to $1$ inch in the model.

steps.title: Find inches per foot
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\frac{19}{4}$: $1$ foot of actual length corresponds to $\frac{1}{19/4} = \frac{4}{19}$ inch in the model.

steps.title: Scale up to x feet
steps.content:
For $x$ feet of actual length, multiply:
$$x \cdot \frac{4}{19} = \frac{4x}{19} \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $\frac{4x}{(19)(12)}$: includes an unnecessary factor of $12$ — eliminate.
  • $\frac{19x}{(12)(4)}$: wrong ratio and extra $12$ — eliminate.
  • $\frac{4x}{19}$: matches the derived expression.
  • $\frac{19x}{4}$: this converts inches to feet, not the reverse — eliminate.
That is the correct answer.
